channel.title: Voices from X
channel.description: Selected long-form posts and articles from the X community.
channel.itunes:author: Daniel Cazzulino
channel.itunes:subtitle: Voices from X
channel.itunes:summary: Selected long-form posts and articles from the X community.

item.title: France Is Technically a Communist Country
item.description:
Brivael Le Pogam argues that France's public spending at 57.2% of GDP exceeds the late USSR, its tax system drives founders and unicorns abroad, even successful scale-up creators live modestly without real sovereignty, public services collapse despite record spending, and an unaccountable EU bureaucracy rules over everything - revealing a soft neo-communist system built on consent rather than coercion.
https://x.com/brivael/status/2069649899365503409
item.itunes:summary:
Brivael Le Pogam argues that France's public spending at 57.2% of GDP exceeds the late USSR, its tax system drives founders and unicorns abroad, even successful scale-up creators live modestly without real sovereignty, public services collapse despite record spending, and an unaccountable EU bureaucracy rules over everything - revealing a soft neo-communist system built on consent rather than coercion.
https://x.com/brivael/status/2069649899365503409

item.title: How USAID Built Color Revolution Infrastructure With NGOs
item.description:
DataRepublican reveals how USAID functioned as a primary tool for regime change by funding and coordinating five key capabilities — independent media, legal infrastructure, election monitoring, activist training, and governance data — across countries like Georgia, and how similar networks now operate inside the United States via private foundations.
https://x.com/DataRepublican/status/2069619851518431312
item.itunes:summary:
DataRepublican reveals how USAID functioned as a primary tool for regime change by funding and coordinating five key capabilities — independent media, legal infrastructure, election monitoring, activist training, and governance data — across countries like Georgia, and how similar networks now operate inside the United States via private foundations.
https://x.com/DataRepublican/status/2069619851518431312

item.title: The Great Liquidation
item.description:
Brivael Le Pogam makes the case that corporate "bullshit" — middle management, bureaucracy, and status games — is a 70-year equilibrium that AI is about to liquidate by collapsing the cost of coordination. He warns founders never to wire their startups into legacy corporate structures that select for the wrong talent and culture.
https://x.com/brivael/status/2069182900990570843
item.itunes:summary:
Brivael Le Pogam makes the case that corporate "bullshit" — middle management, bureaucracy, and status games — is a 70-year equilibrium that AI is about to liquidate by collapsing the cost of coordination. He warns founders never to wire their startups into legacy corporate structures that select for the wrong talent and culture.
https://x.com/brivael/status/2069182900990570843

item.title: Starfall
item.description:
Devon Eriksen describes the vision of 'Starfall' — asteroid mining, space manufacturing and full industrialization off-planet that will allow Earth to be reserved for living, farming and a high standard of living while heavy industry and its infrastructure moves to space where power is free and materials abundant.
https://x.com/Devon_Eriksen_/status/2068928868631667044
item.itunes:summary:
Devon Eriksen describes the vision of 'Starfall' — asteroid mining, space manufacturing and full industrialization off-planet that will allow Earth to be reserved for living, farming and a high standard of living while heavy industry and its infrastructure moves to space where power is free and materials abundant.
https://x.com/Devon_Eriksen_/status/2068928868631667044

item.title: The Valar Reactor
item.description:
Devon Eriksen explains the advantages of fissionable material ('spicy rocks') for Earth-based power versus solar for space, and describes the Valar modular nuclear reactor — compact, transportable power plants the size of a train car that can be mass produced and deployed anywhere.
https://x.com/Devon_Eriksen_/status/2069141400726208736
item.itunes:summary:
Devon Eriksen explains the advantages of fissionable material ('spicy rocks') for Earth-based power versus solar for space, and describes the Valar modular nuclear reactor — compact, transportable power plants the size of a train car that can be mass produced and deployed anywhere.
https://x.com/Devon_Eriksen_/status/2069141400726208736

item.title: The Trick Is To Put The Seeds In The Dirt
item.description:
Devon Eriksen argues that what is natural is theft, robbery, and violence by the strong, and that the free market is only "free" because it is deliberately freed from coercion and maintained by using force to stop thieves and bandits. Without protecting property and future investments, complex civilization collapses into chaos and starvation.
https://x.com/Devon_Eriksen_/status/2068619135303508443
item.itunes:summary:
Devon Eriksen argues that what is natural is theft, robbery, and violence by the strong, and that the free market is only "free" because it is deliberately freed from coercion and maintained by using force to stop thieves and bandits. Without protecting property and future investments, complex civilization collapses into chaos and starvation.
https://x.com/Devon_Eriksen_/status/2068619135303508443

item.title: It's Time to Celebrate Elon Musk
item.description:
Bill Ackman calls it time to celebrate Elon Musk, who has created enormous value for society through SpaceX by driving down the cost of rocket launches and creating a global satellite communication network that brings high speed, low-cost internet to hundreds of millions — and will eventually billions — along with critical advantages for military and defense. SpaceX technologies accelerate wages, wealth, education, business, medicine, compute, and AI. 4,000 employees including hourly workers recently became millionaires. Musk reinvests everything to advance mankind. Socialism would never allow SpaceX; we need more builders like this.
https://x.com/BillAckman/status/2065847551400493112
item.itunes:summary:
Bill Ackman calls it time to celebrate Elon Musk, who has created enormous value for society through SpaceX by driving down the cost of rocket launches and creating a global satellite communication network that brings high speed, low-cost internet to hundreds of millions — and will eventually billions — along with critical advantages for military and defense. SpaceX technologies accelerate wages, wealth, education, business, medicine, compute, and AI. 4,000 employees including hourly workers recently became millionaires. Musk reinvests everything to advance mankind. Socialism would never allow SpaceX; we need more builders like this.
https://x.com/BillAckman/status/2065847551400493112

item.title: Why Elon Musk Is Hated and Demonized by the Media
item.description:
Brivael Le Pogam explains why the press demonizes Elon Musk not despite his achievements building rockets, electric cars and global connectivity, but precisely because those achievements expose the crony systems and gatekeepers he bypasses.
https://x.com/brivael/status/2069203589692805440
item.itunes:summary:
Brivael Le Pogam explains why the press demonizes Elon Musk not despite his achievements building rockets, electric cars and global connectivity, but precisely because those achievements expose the crony systems and gatekeepers he bypasses.
https://x.com/brivael/status/2069203589692805440

item.title: Hope: The Future Is Bright
item.description: Brivael Le Pogam, YC-backed CTO of Argil, makes the case for techno-optimism: builders who act without permission, the French system's hostility to enterprise, and why AI multiplies human capacity rather than rationing the future.
item.itunes:summary: Brivael Le Pogam, YC-backed CTO of Argil, makes the case for techno-optimism: builders who act without permission, the French system's hostility to enterprise, and why AI multiplies human capacity rather than rationing the future.

item.title: SpaceX: Not an IPO — It's a Referendum
item.description: Brivael Le Pogam describes the abnormal retail rush for SpaceX shares. It's not investing or speculation — ordinary people are voting with their money for the camp of builders and makers over the gray bureaucracy of control and permission.
item.itunes:summary: Brivael Le Pogam describes the abnormal retail rush for SpaceX shares. It's not investing or speculation — ordinary people are voting with their money for the camp of builders and makers over the gray bureaucracy of control and permission.
